timeline.story_start: Late summer 2026 in Pittsburgh corridor; move-in day is Friday, 2026-08-28.

seasonal_track.season: Early autumn into first mountain snow

key_events.event: Eli leaves after move-in and initiates emergency-fund theft sequence.
HR termination under job-abandonment policy; benefits sunset set.
I-70 whiteout crash totals sedan and creates injury/medical debt arc.
Nora hard-anchors legal/financial dates (account freeze, filing prep, meal-plan cut).
Insurance proceeds intercepted by lienholder; first Utah wire sent.
Eli returns, signs separation terms, and discloses affair; rules tightened.
June gets direct confession and sets enforceable contact contract; Eli moves to rental apartment.

geography.primary_setting: Interstate route from Pittsburgh through Indiana, Missouri, Kansas, Colorado, Utah, Nevada, then return to Pittsburgh.

key_locations.name: Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
key_locations.details: Home base; legal-separation and boundary-governance site; final apartment phase nearby.

distances: Pittsburgh to St. Louis is roughly 600 miles.
Glenwood Springs to Reno is roughly 850 miles.
Reno to Pittsburgh is over 2,000 miles by surface route.

world_rules: Contemporary realist U.S. setting; no speculative elements.
Financial/legal systems (liens, freezes, filing dates, payroll controls) are enforceable and drive consequence.

environmental_constants: Transit and labor spaces remain sensory-heavy: diesel, bleach, lint, wet concrete, fluorescent buzz.
Seasonal track runs late-August heat through mountain snow into Pittsburgh winter slush.
Administrative paper trails (receipts, ledgers, forms, deadlines) persist as physical continuity anchors.
